小野神社ねんじり棒祭（{{ill|January 6|1月6日}}） |地図 = {{location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=3|lat_sec=18.02|lon_deg=137|lon_min=58|lon_sec=10.32|label=小野神社・矢彦神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja otorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year }}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year }}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year }}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year }}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] ]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. 矢彦：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|16.35|N|137|58|09.44|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=矢彦神社}} |deity = 小野：{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}
矢彦：{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}}、{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} |神体 = |type = 小野：{{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮}}（二宮）、旧{{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}}
矢彦：旧県社 |創建 = 小野：不詳
矢彦：（伝）{{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇}}年間（{{ill|539|539th year }}-{{ill|571|571st year }}?） |本殿 = |alternative names = 小野南北大明神 |札所等 = |festival = 10月第1日曜（八朔祭/田ノ実祭） |神事 = 御柱祭（6th year に1度）
小野神社ねんじり棒祭（{{ill|January 6|1月6日}}） |地図 = {{location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=3|lat_sec=18.02|lon_deg=137|lon_min=58|lon_sec=10.32|label=小野神社・矢彦神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja otorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year }}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year }}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] 矢彦：長野県[[Kamiina District, Nagano|上伊那郡]][[Tatsuno, Nagano|辰野町]]大字小野3267 |coordinates = 小野：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|18.02|N|137|58|10.32|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=小野神社}}

小野神社ねんじり棒祭（{{ill|January 6|1月6日}}）|地図 = {{location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=3|lat_sec=18.02|lon_deg=137|lon_min=58|lon_sec=10.32|label=小野神社・矢彦神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=|12=draft}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja otorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市|12=draft}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町|12=draft}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|12=draft}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神|12=draft}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳|12=draft}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|12=draft}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼|12=draft}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正|12=draft}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子|12=draft}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄|12=draft}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物|12=draft}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直|12=draft}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩|12=draft}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year }}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07|12=draft}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|12=draft}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|12=draft}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

小野神社ねんじり棒祭（{{ill|January 6|1月6日}}）|地図 = {{location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=3|lat_sec=18.02|lon_deg=137|lon_min=58|lon_sec=10.32|label=小野神社・矢彦神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=|12=draft}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja otorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市|12=draft}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町|12=draft}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|12=draft}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神|12=draft}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳|12=draft}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|12=draft}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼|12=draft}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正|12=draft}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子|12=draft}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄|12=draft}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物|12=draft}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直|12=draft}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩|12=draft}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year }}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07|12=draft}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|12=draft}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|12=draft}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

小野神社ねんじり棒祭（{{ill|January 6|1月6日}}）|地図 = {{location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=3|lat_sec=18.02|lon_deg=137|lon_min=58|lon_sec=10.32|label=小野神社・矢彦神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=|12=draft}} }} {{GeoGroup}} [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja otorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市|12=draft}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町|12=draft}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|12=draft}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神|12=draft}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳|12=draft}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|12=draft}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼|12=draft}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正|12=draft}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子|12=draft}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄|12=draft}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物|12=draft}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直|12=draft}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩|12=draft}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year }}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07|12=draft}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|12=draft}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|12=draft}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神|12=draft|12=draft}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|12=draft|12=draft}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼|12=draft|12=draft}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正|12=draft|12=draft}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子|12=draft|12=draft}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄|12=draft|12=draft}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直|12=draft|12=draft}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year }}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07|12=draft|12=draft}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|12=draft|12=draft}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神|12=draft|12=draft}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|12=draft|12=draft}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼|12=draft|12=draft}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正|12=draft|12=draft}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子|12=draft|12=draft}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄|12=draft|12=draft}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直|12=draft|12=draft}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year }}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07|12=draft|12=draft}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|12=draft|12=draft}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神|12=draft|12=draft}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子|12=draft|12=draft}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄|12=draft|12=draft}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市|12=draft|12=draft}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町|12=draft|12=draft}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft|12=draft}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神|12=draft|12=draft}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft|12=draft}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|12=draft|12=draft}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft|12=draft}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼|12=draft|12=draft}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正|12=draft|12=draft}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子|12=draft|12=draft}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄|12=draft|12=draft}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物|12=draft|12=draft}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直|12=draft|12=draft}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft|12=draft}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year }}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07|12=draft|12=draft}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft|12=draft}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|12=draft|12=draft}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]
