小野神社ねんじり棒祭（{{ill|January 6|1月6日}}）|地図 = {{location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=3|lat_sec=18.02|lon_deg=137|lon_min=58|lon_sec=10.32|label=小野神社・矢彦神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} }} {{GeoGroup}} [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja otorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year |lt=Heisei 30|fr|2018}}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

revision.comment: 33 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小野神社・矢彦神社 up to rev 103049591
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小野神社・矢彦神社 |画像 = [[File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) haiden.JPG|280px]]
[[File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) kaguraden.JPG|280px]]
小野神社（上）と矢彦神社（下） |所在地 = 小野：[[長野県]][[塩尻市]]北小野175-1
矢彦：長野県[[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]大字小野3267 |位置 = 小野：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|18.02|N|137|58|10.32|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=小野神社}}
矢彦：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|16.35|N|137|58|09.44|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=矢彦神社}} |祭神 = 小野：[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]
矢彦：[[大国主|大己貴命]]、[[事代主|事代主命]] |神体 = |社格 = 小野：[[信濃国]][[一宮|二宮]]、旧[[県社]]
矢彦：旧県社 |創建 = 小野：不詳
矢彦：（伝）[[欽明天皇]]年間（[[539年]]-[[571年]]?） |本殿 = |別名 = 小野南北大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第1日曜（八朔祭/田ノ実祭） |神事 = 御柱祭（6年に1度）
小野神社ねんじり棒祭（[[1月6日]]） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=3|lat_sec=18.02|lon_deg=137|lon_min=58|lon_sec=10.32|label=小野神社・矢彦神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja otorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
大鳥居
]] '''小野神社・矢彦神社'''（おのじんじゃ・やひこじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]]にある[[神社]]。小野神社は長野県[[塩尻市]]、矢彦神社は長野県[[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]に位置する。 両社は同じ社叢に隣接して鎮座しており、かつては1つの神社であったと伝えるが、現在は別の神社である。小野神社は[[信濃国]][[一宮|二宮]]で{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=269}}、両社とも[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == '''小野神社祭神''' * '''[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]''' （たけみなかたのみこと）{{Sfn|小野神社（明治神社）|1912年}} '''矢彦神社祭神''' * 正殿 ** '''大己貴命''' （おおなむちのみこと：[[大国主|大国主命]]）矢彦神社祭神は境内説明板による。 ** '''[[事代主|事代主命]]''' （ことしろぬしのみこと） * 副殿 ** [[建御名方神|建御名方命]] （たけみなかたのみこと） ** [[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]] （やさかとめのみこと） * 南殿 ** [[天香山命|天香語山命]] （あめのかごやまのみこと） ** 熟穂屋姫命 （うましほやひめのみこと） * 北殿 ** 神倭磐余彦天皇 （かむやまといわれひこのすめらみこと：初代[[神武天皇]]） ** 誉田別天皇 （ほんだわけのすめらみこと：第15代[[応神天皇]]） == 歴史 == === 創建 === 創建年代は不詳。伝承では、建御名方命が諏訪に入ろうとしたところ諏訪には[[洩矢神]]がいて入れなかったため、建御名方命はこの小野の地にしばらく留まったという『日本の神々』小野神社項。。 また矢彦神社社伝では、大己貴命が国作りに勤しんでいた折、御子の事代主命・建御名方命を従えてこの地に立ち寄ったという。そして[[欽明天皇]]年間（[[539年]]-[[571年]]?）、大己貴命と事代主命を正殿に、建御名方命と八坂刀売命を副殿に祀って祭祀の形が整ったと伝える。 創祀の年代等は明らかではないが、当地が古くから交通の要衝であったこと、社宝の神代鉾・[[鉄鐸]]・御正体があることなどから、古い時期から祀られた神社であると見られている。 === 概史 === 国史・神名帳等の文献には小野神社・矢彦神社に関する記載はない。神名の初出は、諏訪大社上社の『祝詞段』（[[嘉禎]]3年（[[1237年]]）成立）にある「小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神」の文言である『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。。[[永禄]]10年（[[1567年]]）の[[生島足島神社|下之郷起請文]]中の[[仁科盛政]]の条では「小野南北大明神」と見える『長野県の地名』矢彦神社項。。また、信濃国の二宮として崇敬されたといわれ、[[天正]]7年（[[1579年]]）の南方久吉契状に「小野二之宮造営」の記載がある『日本中世国家と諸国一宮制』（岩田書院）p. 55。。 両社は古くは1つの神社を成していたといわれるが、小野盆地において[[飯田城 (信濃国)|飯田城]]主[[毛利秀頼]]と[[松本城]]主[[石川数正]]の領地争いがあり、天正19年（[[1591年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉]]の裁定によって、盆地を流れる唐沢川を境に[[筑摩郡]]の北小野村と[[伊那郡]]の南小野村に分けられたことに伴い、神社境内も分割された。江戸時代後期には共に[[天領]]となった（北小野村は松本藩[[預地]]、南小野村は旗本千村氏預地）。 小野神社・矢彦神社とも境内は北小野の地籍であったが、社叢南半分の矢彦神社が南小野の氏神となり、矢彦神社境内は南小野の飛地という扱いとなった。そしてこの状態が現在に至るまで続いている。また門前町は初期[[中山道]]、[[三州街道]]（伊那街道）の宿場町[[小野宿]]となった。 [[明治]]に入り、両社とも[[近代社格制度]]では[[県社]]に列した。 == 境内 == === 社叢の位置と概要 === [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja enkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
社叢遠景
]] 両社が鎮座する小野盆地は、通称を「頼母（たのも）の里」または「憑（たのめ）の里」と言われる。『[[枕草子]]』では「里は」の段で「憑の里」とあるほか、『夫木和歌抄』や[[菅江真澄]]の『委寧能中路』にも記載があり、都人にも知られた地であった『長野県の地名』憑の里項。。これに由来して、現在の小野神社・矢彦神社の例祭も「田ノ実祭」「憑祭」と称される。そのほか、小野盆地には信濃28牧の1つ・小野牧があったことでも知られる。 両社の境内は同一の社叢内にあり、北に小野神社、南に矢彦神社が鎮座する。その社叢は針葉樹と広葉樹が混ざった混交林で、その種類は150種にも及ぶ。社叢の面積は36,326平方メートルで巨木も多く、この地方の天然林を残していることから「矢彦小野神社社叢」として長野県指定天然記念物に指定されている境内説明板。。 なお、小野駅前には両社共通の大鳥居が建てられている（{{Coord|36|02|51.82|N|137|58|13.16|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=大鳥居}}）。 === 小野神社 === [[File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) keidai.JPG|thumb|250px|right|
小野神社境内
]] 小野神社の社殿は、[[寛文]]12年（[[1672年]]）4月の焼失を受け、[[松本藩|松本藩主]]の[[水野忠直]]が同年9月までに再建したものである。本殿2棟と八幡宮本殿が南から並列し、勅使殿がその前方中央に位置する。本殿が2棟あるのは、御柱祭の際に一方からもう一方へ遷座するためで、同形式・同規模の一間社[[流造]]で建てられている。八幡宮本殿はやや小さい一間社流造の見世棚造、勅使殿は切妻造の四脚門である。いずれも現在は銅板葺であるが、古くは柿葺であった。社殿には寛文期に共通する形式とともに、小野神社特有の形式も見られる。これら4棟は長野県宝に指定されている。 [[2018年]]（平成30年）には[[平成30年台風第21号|台風第21号]]で本殿のうち1棟が倒木により大破する被害に遭っている{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html |title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社 |work=[[中日新聞]] |accessdate=2018-09-07}}{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.shinmai.co.jp/news/nagano/20180907/KT180906FTI090019000.php|title=国指定文化財６件被害に被害　台風２１号 |work=[[信濃毎日新聞]] |accessdate=2018-09-07}}。 File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) honden.JPG|本殿2棟、八幡宮本殿、勅使殿（いずれも長野県宝） File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) haiden-2.JPG|拝殿 File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) kaguraden.JPG|神楽殿 File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) torii.JPG|鳥居 === 矢彦神社 === [[File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) keidai.JPG|thumb|250px|right|
矢彦神社境内
{{Smaller|左右には御柱。}}]] 矢彦神社の祭神を祀る正殿・副殿・北殿・南殿は同一の形式・規模で、一直線上に並んでいる。 拝殿と左右の回廊は、天明2年（[[1782年]]）の造営、神楽殿は[[天保]]13年（[[1842年]]）の造営で、いずれも[[立川和四郎|立川流]]の代表建築とされる。勅使殿は江戸時代の造営であるが、室町時代の様式を残した建築である。これら5棟は長野県宝に指定されている。 File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) honden.JPG|正殿・副殿・北殿・南殿 File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) haiden.JPG|拝殿と回廊（長野県宝） File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) chokushiden.JPG|勅使殿（長野県宝） File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) kaguraden-2.JPG|神楽殿（長野県宝） File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) torii.JPG|鳥居 ファイル:昭和15年頃の矢彦神社.jpg|昭和15年頃の矢彦神社 == 摂末社 == '''小野神社''' * 八幡宮 * 稲荷社 * 子安社 * 宗像社 * 天神社 '''矢彦神社''' * 明治宮 - 祭神：[[明治天皇]] == 祭事 == * '''式年御柱大祭''' （6年に1度、卯年と酉年の5月） *: 諏訪大社の[[御柱祭]]（寅年と申年）の翌年に、両社合同で行う。社伝によると、坂上田村麻呂の戦勝祈願が叶ったので、桓武天皇の勅により御柱を建てるようになったことに始まるという。両社とも近隣の山林で御柱を伐り出したのち、御柱を境内まで引く。諏訪大社の勇壮さに比べて、小野神社・矢彦神社の場合にはきらびやかな衣装が特徴であり、古くから「人を見るなら諏訪御柱、綺羅を見るなら小野御柱」と称される。祭りは塩尻市指定無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 * 小野神社ねんじり棒祭 （1月6日） *: 豊年祈年祭で「御田作祭」ともいう。力くらべをする集団的な年占いで、起源は平安時代に遡るともいわれる。祭事では、7本の固い棒を各自の耕地へ入れようとねじり合って競い合う。このような祭事は全国的には殆ど無いといわれ、塩尻市指定無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 * 例大祭（八朔祭/田ノ実祭/憑祭） （10月第1日曜） *: [[例祭]]は両社合同で行う。「八朔祭」の名は、古くは旧暦8月1日に行なわれたことに由来する。水陸様々な供え物を供え儀式を行なう。豊作祈年祭のねんじり棒祭に対して、豊作感謝の祭とされる。 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 長野県宝（有形文化財） ** 小野神社 4棟（建造物） - 平成4年9月10日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3730&seq=0 小野神社]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 ** 矢彦神社 5棟（建造物） - 昭和62年8月17日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121&seq=0 矢彦神社]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 ** 銅造千手観音坐像御正体残闕（工芸品） **: [[神仏習合]]時代の小野神社・矢彦神社の祭祀に関わるとされる仏像。鎌倉時代の作と推定される。平成11年3月18日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038&seq=0 銅造千手観音坐像御正体残闕]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 * 天然記念物 ** 矢彦小野神社社叢 - 昭和35年2月11日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=1473&seq=0 矢彦小野神社社叢]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 === 塩尻市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 小野神社の鐸鉾（さなぎほこ）（神代鉾） **: 古くから祭事に使われたと言い伝えられるが、どういった祭事で用いられたかは明らかでない。鉾には12個の鉄鐸が結び付けられている。本殿向かって左手の池そばに「御鉾様」と称される石（[[磐座]]）があり、中央には穴が空く。このことから、祭儀において鉾をその磐座に立て、神の依代として使用したと見られている。昭和49年8月28日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345&seq=0 小野神社の鐸鉾]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 ** 小野神社の梵鐘 **: [[永禄]]7年（[[1564年]]）、[[武田勝頼]]が戦勝祈願のため鋳造・寄進したもの。いつの頃からか村人が雨乞いのために霧訪山へ引き上げて打ち鳴らし、帰りは転がしたので鐘銘の多くはすり減っている（古文書等によりほぼ全文は明らか）。昭和49年8月28日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2346&seq=0 小野神社の梵鐘]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 小野神社ねんじり棒祭 - 昭和45年1月13日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2336&seq=0 小野神社ねんじり棒祭]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 ** 小野神社御柱祭り - 平成18年1月27日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=5222&seq=0 小野神社御柱祭り]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * 小野神社：[[長野県]][[塩尻市]]北小野175-1 * 矢彦神社：長野県[[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]大字小野3267 '''付属施設''' * 小野神社資料館 - 神宝等を展示。開館時間：例大祭2日間の午前10時から午後4時。 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[中央本線]]（辰野支線） [[小野駅 (長野県)|小野駅]] （徒歩約11分） * バス：[[塩尻市地域振興バス]] 北小野線　中学校入口バス停すぐ '''周辺''' * [[小野宿]] - 初期[[中山道]]、[[三州街道]]（伊那街道）の宿場町。 * しだれ栗森林公園 - 国の天然記念物「[[小野のシダレグリ自生地]]」。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板（長野県教育委員会、塩尻市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「矢彦神社」|ref={{Harvid|矢彦神社（明治神社）|1912年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「小野神社」|ref={{Harvid|小野神社（明治神社）|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/276}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 中』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）参照（矢彦神社は276-277コマ、小野神社は278-279コマ）。 * 『[[日本歴史地名大系]] 長野県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）上伊那郡 矢彦神社項、塩尻市 小野神社項 * 赤羽篤「小野神社」、赤羽篤「矢彦神社」（[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』（[[白水社]]）） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究|publisher=岩田書院|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} == 関連項目 == *[[倉澤清也]] - 矢彦神社祠官 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ono-jinja (Shiojiri)|小野神社}} {{Commonscat|Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno)|矢彦神社}} *[http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ 矢彦神社] - 矢彦神社公式サイト *[http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html 小野神社] - 塩尻市ホームページ * [http://www.tokimeguri.jp/looking/2009/08/25-223140.php 小野神社（県宝）] - 塩尻市観光協会 * [http://kankou.town.tatsuno.nagano.jp/play/info/yahiko-jinja.html 矢彦神社と御柱祭] - 辰野町観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{リダイレクトの所属カテゴリ |redirect1=小野神社 (塩尻市) |1-1=長野県の神社 |1-2=塩尻市の歴史 |1-3=長野県の旧県社 |redirect2=矢彦神社 |2-1=長野県の神社 |2-2=辰野町の歴史 |2-3=長野県の旧県社 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:おのしんしや やひこしんしや}} [[Category:長野県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] [[Category:信濃国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[小野神社・矢彦神社]] to [[Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 小野神社・矢彦神社 |画像 = [[File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) haiden.JPG|280px]]
[[File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) kaguraden.JPG|280px]]
小野神社（上）と矢彦神社（下） |所在地 = 小野：[[長野県]][[塩尻市]]北小野175-1
矢彦：長野県[[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]大字小野3267 |位置 = 小野：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|18.02|N|137|58|10.32|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=小野神社}}
矢彦：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|16.35|N|137|58|09.44|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=矢彦神社}} |祭神 = 小野：[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]
矢彦：[[大国主|大己貴命]]、[[事代主|事代主命]] |神体 = |社格 = 小野：[[信濃国]][[一宮|二宮]]、旧[[県社]]
矢彦：旧県社 |創建 = 小野：不詳
矢彦：（伝）[[欽明天皇]]年間（[[539年]]-[[571年]]?） |本殿 = |別名 = 小野南北大明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月第1日曜（八朔祭/田ノ実祭） |神事 = 御柱祭（6年に1度）
小野神社ねんじり棒祭（[[1月6日]]） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=3|lat_sec=18.02|lon_deg=137|lon_min=58|lon_sec=10.32|label=小野神社・矢彦神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja otorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
大鳥居
]] '''小野神社・矢彦神社'''（おのじんじゃ・やひこじんじゃ）は、[[長野県]]にある[[神社]]。小野神社は長野県[[塩尻市]]、矢彦神社は長野県[[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]に位置する。 両社は同じ社叢に隣接して鎮座しており、かつては1つの神社であったと伝えるが、現在は別の神社である。小野神社は[[信濃国]][[一宮|二宮]]で{{Sfn|中世諸国一宮制|2000年|p=269}}、両社とも[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 祭神 == '''小野神社祭神''' * '''[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]''' （たけみなかたのみこと）{{Sfn|小野神社（明治神社）|1912年}} '''矢彦神社祭神''' * 正殿 ** '''大己貴命''' （おおなむちのみこと：[[大国主|大国主命]]）矢彦神社祭神は境内説明板による。 ** '''[[事代主|事代主命]]''' （ことしろぬしのみこと） * 副殿 ** [[建御名方神|建御名方命]] （たけみなかたのみこと） ** [[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]] （やさかとめのみこと） * 南殿 ** [[天香山命|天香語山命]] （あめのかごやまのみこと） ** 熟穂屋姫命 （うましほやひめのみこと） * 北殿 ** 神倭磐余彦天皇 （かむやまといわれひこのすめらみこと：初代[[神武天皇]]） ** 誉田別天皇 （ほんだわけのすめらみこと：第15代[[応神天皇]]） == 歴史 == === 創建 === 創建年代は不詳。伝承では、建御名方命が諏訪に入ろうとしたところ諏訪には[[洩矢神]]がいて入れなかったため、建御名方命はこの小野の地にしばらく留まったという『日本の神々』小野神社項。。 また矢彦神社社伝では、大己貴命が国作りに勤しんでいた折、御子の事代主命・建御名方命を従えてこの地に立ち寄ったという。そして[[欽明天皇]]年間（[[539年]]-[[571年]]?）、大己貴命と事代主命を正殿に、建御名方命と八坂刀売命を副殿に祀って祭祀の形が整ったと伝える。 創祀の年代等は明らかではないが、当地が古くから交通の要衝であったこと、社宝の神代鉾・[[鉄鐸]]・御正体があることなどから、古い時期から祀られた神社であると見られている。 === 概史 === 国史・神名帳等の文献には小野神社・矢彦神社に関する記載はない。神名の初出は、諏訪大社上社の『祝詞段』（[[嘉禎]]3年（[[1237年]]）成立）にある「小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神」の文言である『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。。[[永禄]]10年（[[1567年]]）の[[生島足島神社|下之郷起請文]]中の[[仁科盛政]]の条では「小野南北大明神」と見える『長野県の地名』矢彦神社項。。また、信濃国の二宮として崇敬されたといわれ、[[天正]]7年（[[1579年]]）の南方久吉契状に「小野二之宮造営」の記載がある『日本中世国家と諸国一宮制』（岩田書院）p. 55。。 両社は古くは1つの神社を成していたといわれるが、小野盆地において[[飯田城 (信濃国)|飯田城]]主[[毛利秀頼]]と[[松本城]]主[[石川数正]]の領地争いがあり、天正19年（[[1591年]]）に[[豊臣秀吉]]の裁定によって、盆地を流れる唐沢川を境に[[筑摩郡]]の北小野村と[[伊那郡]]の南小野村に分けられたことに伴い、神社境内も分割された。江戸時代後期には共に[[天領]]となった（北小野村は松本藩[[預地]]、南小野村は旗本千村氏預地）。 小野神社・矢彦神社とも境内は北小野の地籍であったが、社叢南半分の矢彦神社が南小野の氏神となり、矢彦神社境内は南小野の飛地という扱いとなった。そしてこの状態が現在に至るまで続いている。また門前町は初期[[中山道]]、[[三州街道]]（伊那街道）の宿場町[[小野宿]]となった。 [[明治]]に入り、両社とも[[近代社格制度]]では[[県社]]に列した。 == 境内 == === 社叢の位置と概要 === [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja enkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
社叢遠景
]] 両社が鎮座する小野盆地は、通称を「頼母（たのも）の里」または「憑（たのめ）の里」と言われる。『[[枕草子]]』では「里は」の段で「憑の里」とあるほか、『夫木和歌抄』や[[菅江真澄]]の『委寧能中路』にも記載があり、都人にも知られた地であった『長野県の地名』憑の里項。。これに由来して、現在の小野神社・矢彦神社の例祭も「田ノ実祭」「憑祭」と称される。そのほか、小野盆地には信濃28牧の1つ・小野牧があったことでも知られる。 両社の境内は同一の社叢内にあり、北に小野神社、南に矢彦神社が鎮座する。その社叢は針葉樹と広葉樹が混ざった混交林で、その種類は150種にも及ぶ。社叢の面積は36,326平方メートルで巨木も多く、この地方の天然林を残していることから「矢彦小野神社社叢」として長野県指定天然記念物に指定されている境内説明板。。 なお、小野駅前には両社共通の大鳥居が建てられている（{{Coord|36|02|51.82|N|137|58|13.16|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=大鳥居}}）。 === 小野神社 === [[File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) keidai.JPG|thumb|250px|right|
小野神社境内
]] 小野神社の社殿は、[[寛文]]12年（[[1672年]]）4月の焼失を受け、[[松本藩|松本藩主]]の[[水野忠直]]が同年9月までに再建したものである。本殿2棟と八幡宮本殿が南から並列し、勅使殿がその前方中央に位置する。本殿が2棟あるのは、御柱祭の際に一方からもう一方へ遷座するためで、同形式・同規模の一間社[[流造]]で建てられている。八幡宮本殿はやや小さい一間社流造の見世棚造、勅使殿は切妻造の四脚門である。いずれも現在は銅板葺であるが、古くは柿葺であった。社殿には寛文期に共通する形式とともに、小野神社特有の形式も見られる。これら4棟は長野県宝に指定されている。 [[2018年]]（平成30年）には[[平成30年台風第21号|台風第21号]]で本殿のうち1棟が倒木により大破する被害に遭っている{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html |title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社 |work=[[中日新聞]] |accessdate=2018-09-07}}{{cite news |date=2018-09-07 |url=https://www.shinmai.co.jp/news/nagano/20180907/KT180906FTI090019000.php|title=国指定文化財６件被害に被害　台風２１号 |work=[[信濃毎日新聞]] |accessdate=2018-09-07}}。 File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) honden.JPG|本殿2棟、八幡宮本殿、勅使殿（いずれも長野県宝） File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) haiden-2.JPG|拝殿 File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) kaguraden.JPG|神楽殿 File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) torii.JPG|鳥居 === 矢彦神社 === [[File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) keidai.JPG|thumb|250px|right|
矢彦神社境内
{{Smaller|左右には御柱。}}]] 矢彦神社の祭神を祀る正殿・副殿・北殿・南殿は同一の形式・規模で、一直線上に並んでいる。 拝殿と左右の回廊は、天明2年（[[1782年]]）の造営、神楽殿は[[天保]]13年（[[1842年]]）の造営で、いずれも[[立川和四郎|立川流]]の代表建築とされる。勅使殿は江戸時代の造営であるが、室町時代の様式を残した建築である。これら5棟は長野県宝に指定されている。 File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) honden.JPG|正殿・副殿・北殿・南殿 File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) haiden.JPG|拝殿と回廊（長野県宝） File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) chokushiden.JPG|勅使殿（長野県宝） File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) kaguraden-2.JPG|神楽殿（長野県宝） File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) torii.JPG|鳥居 ファイル:昭和15年頃の矢彦神社.jpg|昭和15年頃の矢彦神社 == 摂末社 == '''小野神社''' * 八幡宮 * 稲荷社 * 子安社 * 宗像社 * 天神社 '''矢彦神社''' * 明治宮 - 祭神：[[明治天皇]] == 祭事 == * '''式年御柱大祭''' （6年に1度、卯年と酉年の5月） *: 諏訪大社の[[御柱祭]]（寅年と申年）の翌年に、両社合同で行う。社伝によると、坂上田村麻呂の戦勝祈願が叶ったので、桓武天皇の勅により御柱を建てるようになったことに始まるという。両社とも近隣の山林で御柱を伐り出したのち、御柱を境内まで引く。諏訪大社の勇壮さに比べて、小野神社・矢彦神社の場合にはきらびやかな衣装が特徴であり、古くから「人を見るなら諏訪御柱、綺羅を見るなら小野御柱」と称される。祭りは塩尻市指定無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 * 小野神社ねんじり棒祭 （1月6日） *: 豊年祈年祭で「御田作祭」ともいう。力くらべをする集団的な年占いで、起源は平安時代に遡るともいわれる。祭事では、7本の固い棒を各自の耕地へ入れようとねじり合って競い合う。このような祭事は全国的には殆ど無いといわれ、塩尻市指定無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 * 例大祭（八朔祭/田ノ実祭/憑祭） （10月第1日曜） *: [[例祭]]は両社合同で行う。「八朔祭」の名は、古くは旧暦8月1日に行なわれたことに由来する。水陸様々な供え物を供え儀式を行なう。豊作祈年祭のねんじり棒祭に対して、豊作感謝の祭とされる。 == 文化財 == === 長野県指定文化財 === * 長野県宝（有形文化財） ** 小野神社 4棟（建造物） - 平成4年9月10日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3730&seq=0 小野神社]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 ** 矢彦神社 5棟（建造物） - 昭和62年8月17日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121&seq=0 矢彦神社]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 ** 銅造千手観音坐像御正体残闕（工芸品） **: [[神仏習合]]時代の小野神社・矢彦神社の祭祀に関わるとされる仏像。鎌倉時代の作と推定される。平成11年3月18日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038&seq=0 銅造千手観音坐像御正体残闕]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 * 天然記念物 ** 矢彦小野神社社叢 - 昭和35年2月11日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=1473&seq=0 矢彦小野神社社叢]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 === 塩尻市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 小野神社の鐸鉾（さなぎほこ）（神代鉾） **: 古くから祭事に使われたと言い伝えられるが、どういった祭事で用いられたかは明らかでない。鉾には12個の鉄鐸が結び付けられている。本殿向かって左手の池そばに「御鉾様」と称される石（[[磐座]]）があり、中央には穴が空く。このことから、祭儀において鉾をその磐座に立て、神の依代として使用したと見られている。昭和49年8月28日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345&seq=0 小野神社の鐸鉾]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 ** 小野神社の梵鐘 **: [[永禄]]7年（[[1564年]]）、[[武田勝頼]]が戦勝祈願のため鋳造・寄進したもの。いつの頃からか村人が雨乞いのために霧訪山へ引き上げて打ち鳴らし、帰りは転がしたので鐘銘の多くはすり減っている（古文書等によりほぼ全文は明らか）。昭和49年8月28日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2346&seq=0 小野神社の梵鐘]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 小野神社ねんじり棒祭 - 昭和45年1月13日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2336&seq=0 小野神社ねんじり棒祭]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 ** 小野神社御柱祭り - 平成18年1月27日指定[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=5222&seq=0 小野神社御柱祭り]（公益財団法人 八十二文化財団）。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * 小野神社：[[長野県]][[塩尻市]]北小野175-1 * 矢彦神社：長野県[[上伊那郡]][[辰野町]]大字小野3267 '''付属施設''' * 小野神社資料館 - 神宝等を展示。開館時間：例大祭2日間の午前10時から午後4時。 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[中央本線]]（辰野支線） [[小野駅 (長野県)|小野駅]] （徒歩約11分） * バス：[[塩尻市地域振興バス]] 北小野線　中学校入口バス停すぐ '''周辺''' * [[小野宿]] - 初期[[中山道]]、[[三州街道]]（伊那街道）の宿場町。 * しだれ栗森林公園 - 国の天然記念物「[[小野のシダレグリ自生地]]」。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 境内説明板（長野県教育委員会、塩尻市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所編|author=|year=1912|chapter=|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「矢彦神社」|ref={{Harvid|矢彦神社（明治神社）|1912年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「小野神社」|ref={{Harvid|小野神社（明治神社）|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/276}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 中』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）参照（矢彦神社は276-277コマ、小野神社は278-279コマ）。 * 『[[日本歴史地名大系]] 長野県の地名』（[[平凡社]]）上伊那郡 矢彦神社項、塩尻市 小野神社項 * 赤羽篤「小野神社」、赤羽篤「矢彦神社」（[[谷川健一]] 編『日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 9 美濃・飛騨・信濃』（[[白水社]]）） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究|publisher=岩田書院|isbn=978-4872941708|ref={{Harvid|中世諸国一宮制|2000年}}}} == 関連項目 == *[[倉澤清也]] - 大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year |lt=Heisei 30|fr|2018}}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|小野神社・矢彦神社|version=103049591|comment=Imported full ja history}}

矢彦：長野県[[Kamiina District, Nagano|上伊那郡]][[Tatsuno, Nagano|辰野町]]大字小野3267 |coordinates = 小野：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|18.02|N|137|58|10.32|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=小野神社}}
矢彦：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|16.35|N|137|58|09.44|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=矢彦神社}} |deity = 小野：{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}

大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市|12=draft}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町|12=draft}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|12=draft}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|12=draft}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神|12=draft}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=draft}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳|12=draft}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|12=draft}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼|12=draft}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正|12=draft}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子|12=draft}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄|12=draft}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物|12=draft}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直|12=draft}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩|12=draft}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year|lt=Heisei 30|fr|2018|12=draft}}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|12=draft}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|12=draft}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|小野神社・矢彦神社|version=103049591|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|12=draft}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year|lt=Heisei 30|fr|2018|12=draft}}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|12=draft}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|12=draft}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|12=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|12=draft}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小野神社・矢彦神社|version=103049591|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = 小野神社・矢彦神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Ono-jinja (Shiojiri) haiden.JPG|280px]]
[[File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) kaguraden.JPG|280px]]
小野神社（上）と矢彦神社（下） |location = 小野：{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft}}[[Shiojiri|塩尻市]]北小野175-1
矢彦：長野県[[Kamiina District, Nagano|上伊那郡]][[Tatsuno, Nagano|辰野町]]大字小野3267 |coordinates = 小野：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|18.02|N|137|58|10.32|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=小野神社}}
矢彦：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|16.35|N|137|58|09.44|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=矢彦神社}} |deity = 小野：{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft}}
矢彦：{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft}}、{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft}} |神体 = |type = 小野：{{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft}}（二宮）、旧{{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft}}
矢彦：旧県社 |創建 = 小野：不詳
矢彦：（伝）{{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft}}年間（{{ill|539|539th year }}-{{ill|571|571st year|qq=draft}}?） |本殿 = |alternative names = 小野南北大明神 |札所等 = |festival = 10月第1日曜（八朔祭/田ノ実祭） |神事 = 御柱祭（6th year に1度）
小野神社ねんじり棒祭（{{ill|January 6|1月6日}}）|地図 = {{location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=3|lat_sec=18.02|lon_deg=137|lon_min=58|lon_sec=10.32|label=小野神社・矢彦神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=|qq=draft}} }} {{GeoGroup}} [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja otorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市|qq=draft}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町|qq=draft}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神|qq=draft}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳|qq=draft}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year }}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year }}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼|qq=draft}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正|qq=draft}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子|qq=draft}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄|qq=draft}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物|qq=draft}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直|qq=draft}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩|qq=draft}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year|lt=Heisei 30|fr|2018|qq=draft}}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|qq=draft}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|qq=draft}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小野神社・矢彦神社|version=103049591|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物|qq=draft}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year }} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直|qq=draft}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩|qq=draft}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year|lt=Heisei 30|fr|2018|qq=draft}}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year }}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|qq=draft}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|qq=draft}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小野神社・矢彦神社|version=103049591|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

[[File:Yahiko-jinja (Tatsuno) kaguraden.JPG|280px]]
小野神社（上）と矢彦神社（下） |location = 小野：{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}[[Shiojiri|塩尻市]]北小野175-1
矢彦：長野県[[Kamiina District, Nagano|上伊那郡]][[Tatsuno, Nagano|辰野町]]大字小野3267 |coordinates = 小野：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|18.02|N|137|58|10.32|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=小野神社}}
矢彦：{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|03|16.35|N|137|58|09.44|E|region:JP-20_type:landmark|name=矢彦神社}} |deity = 小野：{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}
矢彦：{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}、{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} |神体 = |type = 小野：{{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}{{ill|Ichinomiya|ja|一宮|1=Ichinomiya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinomiya|qq=}}（二宮）、旧{{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}}
矢彦：旧県社 |創建 = 小野：不詳
矢彦：（伝）{{ill|Emperor Kinmei|ja|欽明天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Kinmei|qq=}}年間（{{ill|539|539th year|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/539|qq=}}-{{ill|571|571st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/571|qq=}}?） |本殿 = |alternative names = 小野南北大明神 |札所等 = |festival = 10月第1日曜（八朔祭/田ノ実祭） |神事 = 御柱祭（6th year に1度）
小野神社ねんじり棒祭（{{ill|January 6|1月6日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 6|qq=}}）|地図 = {{location map|Japan Nagano|lat_deg=36|lat_min=3|lat_sec=18.02|lon_deg=137|lon_min=58|lon_sec=10.32|label=小野神社・矢彦神社|position=right|width=200|float=center|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|caption=|qq=draft}} }} {{GeoGroup}} [[File:Ono-jinja, Yahiko-jinja otorii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|
大鳥居
]] '''Ono-jinja and Yahiko-jinja''' (小野神社・矢彦神社) are a pair of [[Shinto shrine]]s located in {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagano Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiojiri|qq=}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno, Nagano|qq=}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya|qq=}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1237|qq=}}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku|qq=}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tensho|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1591|qq=}}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Hideyori|qq=}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Kazumasa|qq=}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makura no Sōshi|qq=}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fubokuwakashō|qq=}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Monument (Japan)|qq=}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1672|qq=}} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadanaka|qq=}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Domain|qq=}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onbashira Festival|qq=}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year|lt=Heisei 30|fr|2018|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018|qq=}}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1782|qq=}}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1842|qq=}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster (zodiac)|qq=}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小野神社・矢彦神社|version=103049591|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]

Ono-jinja is in {{ill|Shiojiri|ja|塩尻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiojiri|qq=}}, while Yahiko-jinja is in {{ill|Tatsuno, Nagano|ja|辰野町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuno, Nagano|qq=}}. Both shrines are situated within the same sacred grove and are said to have once been a single shrine. Currently, they are distinct entities. Ono-jinja is considered the [[Ninomiya]] (Ni-no-miya) of {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}}, and both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == '''Ono-jinja deity''' * '''{{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}''' (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) '''Yahiko-jinja Deities''' * '''Main Sanctuary (Seiden)''' ** '''{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}''' (Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto) ** '''{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}}''' (Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto) * '''Sub Sanctuary (Fukuden)''' ** {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto) ** {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=}} (Yasakatome-no-Mikoto) * '''South Sanctuary (Nanden)''' ** {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天香語山命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|qq=}} (Ame-no-Kagoyama-no-Mikoto) ** Umashihoya-hime (熟穂屋姫命) * '''North Sanctuary (Hokuden)''' ** Kamuyamato-iwarehiko (神倭磐余彦天皇: Emperor Jimmu) ** Hondawake (誉田別天皇: Emperor Ōjin) == History == === Establishment === The exact founding date of the shrines is unknown. Legends state that {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} stayed in this area temporarily after being unable to enter Suwa due to the presence of {{ill|Moreya|ja|洩矢神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moreya|qq=}}.『日本の神々』小野神社項。 According to Yahiko-jinja’s tradition, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} stopped in this area during his nation-building journey, accompanied by his sons {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}. Further legends describe how the shrines’ rituals and structure were formalized during the reign of Emperor Kinmei (539–571). === Later History === The shrines do not appear in early historical records such as the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} or the {{ill|Jinmyōchō|ja|神名帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. However, they are first mentioned in the {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} text "Norito-dan" (祝詞段), compiled in {{ill|1237|嘉禎3rd year|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1237|qq=}}, which refers to "Ono-Wayahiko Hokubou Nanbou Suewakamiya Daimyōjin" (小野ワヤヒコ北方南方末若宮大明神).『日本の神々』矢彦神社項。 During the {{ill|Eiroku|ja|永禄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eiroku|qq=}} period (1567), they were called "Ono Nanboku Daimyōjin" (小野南北大明神) in the Shimo-no-Go Okisho Mon (下之郷起請文), and during the {{ill|Tensho|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tensho|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qq=}} era (1579), there are records of shrine renovations noted as "Ono Ninomiyazōei" (小野二之宮造営).{{Cite book|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会編|title=日本中世国家と諸国一宮制|publisher=岩田書院|year=2000|page=55}} In {{ill|1591|天正19th year|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1591|qq=}}, following a dispute over the Ono basin between {{ill|Mōri Hideyori|ja|毛利秀頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mōri Hideyori|qq=}} of Iida Castle and {{ill|Ishikawa Kazumasa|ja|石川数正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa Kazumasa|qq=}} of Matsumoto Castle, the basin was divided into northern (Kitano-Ono Village) and southern (Minamino-Ono Village) regions, with the shrines physically divided as a result. In the modern era, both shrines were classified as {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. == Shrine Grounds == === Sacred Forest (社叢) === The shrines share the same sacred forest (社叢), located in the Ono basin, which is also referred to as the "Tanomo no Sato" (頼母の里) or "Tanome no Sato" (憑の里). The forest has been mentioned in literary works such as the {{ill|Makura no Sōshi|ja|枕草子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makura no Sōshi|qq=}} and in other classical texts like the {{ill|Fubokuwakashō|ja|夫木和歌抄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fubokuwakashō|qq=}}.『長野県の地名』憑の里項。 This sacred forest, known for its biodiversity, contains over 150 species of trees, including significant examples of broadleaf and coniferous species. It has been designated as a {{ill|Natural Monument (Japan)|ja|天然記念物|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natural Monument (Japan)|qq=}}, known as the "Yahiko-Ono Shrine Sacred Forest" (矢彦小野神社社叢). === Ono-jinja === The shrine buildings at Ono-jinja were rebuilt in {{ill|1672|寛文12nd year|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1672|qq=}} under the patronage of {{ill|Mizuno Tadanaka|ja|水野忠直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuno Tadanaka|qq=}}, the lord of {{ill|Matsumoto Domain|ja|松本藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsumoto Domain|qq=}}. Key structures include: * Two {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} honden (main shrines), used alternately during the {{ill|Onbashira Festival|ja|御柱祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onbashira Festival|qq=}}. * A Hachiman Shrine honden. * A Chokushiden (勅使殿), located in the central area. In {{ill|2018|平成30th year|lt=Heisei 30|fr|2018|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018|qq=}}, Typhoon No. 21 caused severe damage to one of the honden, which was later restored.{{Cite news|date=2018-09-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180907144742/http://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/nagano/20180907/CK2018090702000012.html|title=県宝本殿、倒木で大破　塩尻・小野神社|work=[[Chunichi Shimbun]]|accessdate=2018-09-07}} === Yahiko-jinja === Yahiko-jinja features four sanctuaries (正殿、副殿、北殿、南殿), each identical in design, aligned in a straight line. Other structures include: * The Haiden (拝殿), built in {{ill|1782|天明2nd year|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1782|qq=}}. * A Kagura-den (神楽殿), built in {{ill|1842|天保13rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1842|qq=}}. * A Chokushiden, constructed in the {{ill|Muromachi period|ja|室町時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muromachi period|qq=}} style. == Festivals == * '''Shikinen Onbashira Taisha Festival''' (式年御柱大祭) - Held every six years in the years of the {{ill|Rabbit|卯|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|2=ja|3=卯|4=fr|5=Lapin (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Konijn (astrologie)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|1=Rabbit (zodiac)|lt=Rabbit (卯)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit|13=User:Immanuelle/Rabbit (zodiac)|qq=}} and {{ill|Rooster|酉|1=Rooster (zodiac)|2=ja|3=酉|4=fr|5=Coq (astrologie chinoise)|6=nl|7=Haan (astrologie)|lt=Rooster (酉)|1=Rooster (zodiac)|lt=Rooster (酉)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster|13=User:Immanuelle/Rooster (zodiac)|qq=}}. This festival involves the raising of sacred pillars and reflects the vibrant traditional culture of the region. * '''Nenjiri-bō Festival''' (ねんじり棒祭) - A competitive ritual dating back to the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} that involves strength contests with sacred wooden rods. This is designated as a cultural property by Shiojiri City. * '''festival (八朔祭/田ノ実祭)''' - Celebrated jointly by both shrines on the first Sunday of October to offer thanks for the harvest season. == Cultural Properties == === Prefectural Cultural Properties === * '''Buildings (長野県宝)''' ** Ono-jinja: 4 structures including honden and Chokushiden. ** Yahiko-jinja: 5 structures including haiden and Kagura-den.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=3121 Yahiko-jinja Cultural Properties] * '''Artifacts''' ** "Takuhoko" (鐸鉾), a sacred relic from Ono-jinja.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=2345 Ono-jinja Takuhoko]. ** Bronze Senju Kannon statue associated with the shrine's ancient Buddhist practices.[http://www.82bunka.or.jp/bunkazai/detail.php?no=4038 Senju Kannon Statue] == Access == '''Address:''' * Ono-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Shiojiri City, Kitaono 175-1 * Yahiko-jinja: Nagano Prefecture, Kamiina District, Tatsuno Town, Ono 3267 '''Transportation:''' * By train: {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|qq=}} Chūō Main Line, Tatsuno Branch, Ono Station (11-minute walk). * By bus: Shiojiri Community Bus (北小野線), stop at "Chūgakkō-iriguchi." == External Links == * [http://www.yahikojinja.jp/ Yahiko-jinja Official Website] (矢彦神社) * [http://www.city.shiojiri.lg.jp/kanko/sogokankoannai/meisyokyuseki/onojinjya.html Ono-jinja Page] - Shiojiri City Official Site ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|小野神社・矢彦神社|version=103049591|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:小野神社・矢彦神社]] [[sn:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]] [[en:Ono Shrine & Yahiko Shrine]]
